Trade plan: SMALL SIZE Fri NY session — month-end + CME launch volatility; take profits before 4:30 PM CT weekend-gap mechanism dies; DON'T carry full size into FIRST 24/7 weekend.

Combined with the recent price action and live headlines (see LIVE FEED), the regime is risk-off until a sweep-and-reclaim candle prints. No new longs until F&G stops falling AND BTC reclaims its 24h high.

insights.title: CME 24/7 Crypto Futures Launch TODAY 4:30 PM CT — Last 'CME Gap' Weekend Ends
insights.body: Verified cmegroup.com: CME launches 24/7 trading for BTC, ETH, AVAX, SUI futures + options TODAY Fri May 29 at 4:30 PM CT (CORRECTION from earlier reports listing 4:00 PM CT). Daily 4:00-4:02 PM CT maintenance window M-F + Sat 2-4 AM CT. The weekend-gap fade trade dies tonight — 77% of historical CME gaps filled per CryptoTimes; that strategy retires. Arb desks unwinding weekend-gap positioning into Fri NY close = unusual volume + spread behavior expected. First 24/7 weekend is structurally UNPRICED — don't carry full size into it. Asymmetric play: if BTC reclaims $74K + ETH holds $2K with volume into the launch, this becomes the high-conviction long basket setup; failure = weekend gap-down with no rest mechanism.

insights.title: PCE Actual — Cool m/m But Sticky YoY = Stagflation Tone Confirmed
insights.body: April Core PCE printed +0.2% m/m vs +0.3% consensus (verified bea.gov via Benzinga) — a cool monthly print. BUT core YoY ACCELERATED to 3.3% (highest since Oct 2023, vs March 3.2%). Headline 3.8% YoY (highest since May 2023). Q1 GDP 2nd estimate REVISED DOWN to +1.6% from +2.0% advance — stagflation whisper. Bond market read the YoY tick higher; Treasury yields rose; July rate-cut odds softened. The market got a mixed read = no clear directional bias for risk assets. Combined with US-Iran strike headlines Thu (which drove BTC to 6-week low), the tape ignored the cool m/m and stayed risk-off. Stagflation is the worst macro mix for risk assets; rotation into BTC (store-of-value) over alts is the structural play if this regime holds.

insights.title: SUI Mainnet Outage Thu — 2nd in 5 Months Damages Institutional Narrative
insights.body: Verified blog.sui.io / CoinDesk / Decrypt: SUI mainnet halted block production ~5 hours starting Thu May 28 ~07:15 PDT, recovered same day. This is the SECOND outage in 5 months — January 2026 had a 6-hour consensus divergence. For an L1 trying to compete with Aptos/Solana on the institutional access narrative (3 US spot ETFs, CME futures, Nasdaq treasury holders), this is structural damage, not transient. SUI -8% intraday on the outage with partial recovery; Thu close ~$0.92. Bias SHORT into bounces toward $0.95. The ETF-flow risk is the next domino: a multi-week outage narrative could trigger ETF outflows we haven't seen yet. Below $0.905 fresh low = $0.85 cycle extreme target in play.

insights.title: XRP Is Now Verifiably The Strongest Major — May ETF $118M MTD

XRP is the basket's TOP-priority HIGH ticker today.

insights.title: Friday Tactical — Month-End + CME Launch + First-24/7 Weekend Asymmetric
insights.body: Today is month-end AND CME 24/7 launch AND first weekend without CME-gap mechanism. Three unusual catalysts collide. Expect month-end rebalancing flows pre-launch (window-dressing into 4:00 PM ET equity close, then crypto reaction). Take profits before 4:30 PM CT — the weekend-gap arb strategy that 77% of traders relied on dies at that exact moment. DON'T carry full size into the first 24/7 weekend — the behavior is structurally unpriced and could go in either direction violently. The asymmetric play: if BTC reclaims $74K + ETH holds $2K + ETF flips green Fri, this is the high-conviction long basket into the weekend. If BTC fails $74K = weekend gap-down likely with no rest mechanism = SHORT into 4:30 PM CT close.
